name: Amber Grant for Women
description: Monthly $10,000 grants for women-owned small businesses across multiple categories (Amber, Startup, and Business Category), with year-end winners receiving an additional $50,000. A $15 one-time application fee considers applicants for all monthly categories.
notes: Requires a $15 application fee. Applications accepted year-round with monthly deadlines.

name: FedEx Small Business Grant Contest
description: Annual contest awarding 12 U.S. small businesses cash grants (one $50,000 grand prize, one $30,000 second place, ten $15,000 third-place awards) plus FedEx Office print services. Recurs annually; the 2026 cycle closed in March.
notes: Requires an active FedEx business shipping account. Nonprofits and franchises ineligible. Recurs annually.

name: Comcast RISE
description: Provides small businesses a $5,000 monetary grant plus a technology makeover, marketing/advertising support, and business consultation. Recurs annually; applications typically open mid-year.
notes: Business must be 2+ years old with 100 or fewer employees. 2026 deadline/regions not confirmed in sources; status estimated closed as of 2026-06-11. Recurs.

name: SBA Growth Accelerator Fund Competition (GAFC)
description: Two-stage competition awarding entrepreneur support organizations (accelerators, universities, nonprofits) Stage One (~$50,000) and Stage Two (~$150,000) to strengthen R&D-focused small business innovation ecosystems. Recurs annually.
notes: Funds ecosystem-building organizations rather than individual businesses. Award amounts vary by fiscal year; FY2025 figures cited. Recurs annually.

name: Grants for Arts Projects
description: Federal grants supporting arts projects by nonprofit 501(c)(3) organizations, ranging $10,000-$100,000 (subgranting agencies up to $150,000). Requires a 1:1 nonfederal match. Recurs with two deadlines annually.
notes: Applicant must have 5+ years of arts programming. Second FY2026 deadline is July 9, 2026; first deadline (Feb 12, 2026) has passed.

name: Small Business Growth Fund
description: Grants of $5,000-$25,000 for U.S. for-profit small businesses with $5,000-$5,000,000 in annual gross revenue. Hello Alice runs multiple rolling and partner-sponsored grant programs throughout the year.
notes: Hello Alice hosts many concurrent partner grants with varying deadlines; the flagship Growth Fund is the reference here. No application fee.

name: NASE Growth Grant
description: Awards up to $4,000 to NASE member small businesses to fund a specific business need such as marketing, equipment, or hiring. Applications accepted year-round with quarterly review cycles.
notes: Requires NASE membership in good standing. Monthly members must wait 90 days after joining.

name: Value-Added Producer Grant (VAPG)
description: Federal grants helping agricultural producers develop value-added products from raw commodities, with planning grants up to $50,000 and working capital grants up to $200,000. Requires a 1:1 match. Recurs annually.
notes: FY2026 application window (Feb 17-Apr 22, 2026) has closed; program recurs annually.

name: Military Entrepreneur Challenge
description: Pitch competition awarding veteran, military spouse, and Gold Star family entrepreneurs capital grants ($15,000 first, $6,000 second, $4,000 third) plus in-kind legal services. Recurs annually with regional events.
notes: Eligibility limited to veterans, military spouses, and Gold Star families. Held as recurring regional competitions; specific 2026 event dates vary.

name: Patagonia Corporate Environmental Grants
description: Grants of roughly $5,000-$20,000 to small grassroots 501(c)(3) environmental organizations running direct-action campaigns to protect local habitats and frontline communities. Recurs annually (fiscal year May 1-Apr 30).
notes: One proposal per group per fiscal year. FY2026 deadline of Apr 30, 2026 has passed; recurs annually.

name: Awesome Foundation Micro-Grants
description: No-strings-attached $1,000 micro-grants awarded monthly by autonomous local chapters to individuals, groups, and organizations for projects spanning arts, technology, and community development.
notes: Open to anyone (individuals, businesses, nonprofits). Deadlines and themes vary by chapter; generally monthly.

name: Scale-Up Pitch Challenge
description: Annual pitch competition awarding non-dilutive cash prizes up to $50,000 to early-stage Black-founded startups (in operation no more than ten years). Recurs annually, typically culminating at the NBMBAA conference.
notes: Founder must be Black, a U.S. citizen, and 18+. Recurs annually; specific 2026 deadline not confirmed in sources.

name: The Coramino Fund Grants
description: $10,000 grants for small businesses (5 years or younger, up to 30 employees) in Atlanta, Chicago, Detroit, Houston, and Los Angeles, co-founded by Kevin Hart. Recurs in rounds. The 2026 round closed April 6.
notes: Geographically limited to five cities. 2026 round closed; program runs in recurring rounds.

name: Spark Good Local Grants
description: Local cash grants of $250-$5,000 from Walmart stores, Sam's Clubs, and distribution centers to nonprofits serving the local community in areas like animal welfare, elder services, and clean-up. Runs in three application cycles annually.
notes: Requires a verified Spark Good account. 2026 final cycle (Cycle 3) runs Aug 1-Nov 30, 2026; status open reflects an active cycle as of 2026-06-11.

name: Verizon Small Business Digital Ready National Grant
description: $10,000 grants for U.S. for-profit small businesses, awarded to 10 businesses monthly June-December 2026. Applicants must complete two courses or events on the Digital Ready platform to unlock the application.
notes: Application stays under consideration for the full year once submitted. Free to apply; requires completing platform coursework.

name: SBIR/STTR Phase I Awards
description: Federal R&D grants for U.S. small businesses pursuing innovative technology with commercial potential, administered across agencies (NSF, NIH, DOE, USDA, etc.) with varying Phase I ceilings up to roughly $323,090.
notes: Each participating agency sets its own award amounts and solicitation deadlines; the cited ceiling is the SBA guideline as of April 2026.

name: IFundWomen Universal Grant Application
description: A single rolling application that matches women-owned and other small businesses to partner grant opportunities ranging $1,000-$25,000 (some partner awards higher), plus coaching and funding resources.
notes: Free to apply. Award amounts and criteria depend on the matched partner grant; database matching is ongoing.

name: Neighborhood Builders
description: Provides selected 501(c)(3) nonprofits $200,000 in general operating support over two years plus leadership training for the executive director and an emerging leader. Recurs annually in participating markets.
notes: 2026 application window is June 1-July 1, 2026, so it is open as of 2026-06-11. Available in select markets only.

name: Our Town
description: Supports creative placemaking projects that integrate arts, culture, and design to advance local community development outcomes. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits, tribal communities, and units of government, applying through the Grants for Arts Projects portal under the Design & Our Town discipline.
notes: Requires a 1:1 nonfederal cost share/match. Two cycles per year (Feb and July); July 9, 2026 is the Part 1 intent-to-apply deadline.

name: Veteran Housing Grants Program
description: Funds new construction or rehabilitation of permanent supportive housing for veterans. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits in good standing with the IRS for at least five years with prior veteran housing development experience.
notes: Proposals accepted year-round with decisions three times annually; the next cycle deadline is July 3, 2026. Requires a minimum operating budget of $300,000.

name: Grants for Organizations (Charitable Investments Program)
description: Supports programs that improve the health and well-being of veterans, service members, military families, and caregivers, addressing areas such as mental health, employment, housing, and food insecurity. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits meeting financial and program-spend criteria.
notes: Applications accepted year-round and reviewed twice a year (spring and fall). Requires two years of gross receipts over $50,000 and filed Form 990/990EZ.

name: Cisco Technology Grant Program
description: Provides donated and deeply discounted Cisco and Meraki networking and communications technology to qualifying nonprofits. Open to 501(c)(3) organizations that primarily serve economically disadvantaged people or focus on environmental conservation, with an annual operating budget of $20 million or less.
notes: Smaller, routine requests are processed on a rolling basis through TechSoup; only an administrative fee applies. Advocacy and grantmaking organizations are ineligible.

name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grants
description: Funds community-driven solutions advancing racial equity and the well-being of vulnerable children and families across education, health, food security, and economic opportunity. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits, public entities, schools, and other legal entities doing charitable work.
notes: Year-round rolling applications via the Fluxx portal; no fixed deadlines. Funding priorities center on communities of systemic disadvantage.

name: Five Star and Urban Waters Restoration Grant Program
description: Provides financial assistance to local partnerships for wetland, riparian, forest, and coastal habitat restoration, stormwater management, and community stewardship with a focus on water quality and watersheds. Open to 501(c) nonprofits, educational institutions, and government and tribal organizations.
notes: 2026 RFP active; recurs annually with deadlines typically in late January. Requires a 1:0.75 match to be competitive.

name: Community Food Projects Competitive Grants Program (CFPCGP)
funder: USDA National Institute of Food and Agriculture
description: Funds projects that promote community food security and self-reliance, including food access and planning projects for low-income communities. Open to private nonprofit entities, public food program service providers, and tribal organizations.
notes: FY2026 deadline was October 30, 2025; recurs annually. Requires a 1:1 match. Planning Projects $25,000–$50,000; Community Food Projects up to $400,000 over four years.

name: Mary Kay Ash Foundation Domestic Violence Shelter Grant
description: Awards operating grants to emergency domestic violence shelters providing life-saving services to women and children seeking safety from abuse, with at least one grant in every U.S. state each year. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits operating 5+ years that primarily serve women survivors.
notes: Annual cycle: applications open January 1 and close April 30; awards announced in fall. Currently closed as of June 2026.

name: NEA Big Read
funder: National Endowment for the Arts (administered by Arts Midwest)
description: Provides matching grants to host community-wide reading programs built around a single book to encourage dialogue and connection. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits and other tax-exempt entities including libraries, museums, universities, and tribal organizations.
notes: 2026-27 intent-to-apply (Jan 15, 2026) and full application (Jan 29, 2026) deadlines have passed; recurs annually. Requires a 1:1 cost-share match.

name: AARP Community Challenge
description: Funds quick-action projects that make communities more livable for residents of all ages, especially those 50+, across public places, transportation, housing, digital connections, and disaster resilience. Open to 501(c)(3), (c)(4), and (c)(6) nonprofits and government entities.
notes: 2026 application closed March 4, 2026; recurs annually. Larger capacity-building and demonstration grants can exceed $15,000.

name: State Farm Neighborhood Assist
description: A crowd-sourced philanthropic program awarding grants to nonprofit causes for neighborhood improvement projects, with winners selected by public vote. Open to U.S. 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations and eligible public entities.
notes: Recurs annually in spring; 2026 winners announced April 12, 2026, so the cycle is currently closed. 100 causes funded at $25,000 each in 2026.

name: Ben & Jerry's Foundation National Grassroots Organizing Grant
description: Provides unrestricted general operating support to small, constituent-led grassroots organizations using collective-action organizing to advance social and environmental justice. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits (or those with a fiscal sponsor) with annual budgets under $350,000.
notes: 2026 deadline was February 25, 2026; recurs annually. The foundation no longer uses a year-round rolling process.

name: Whole Foods Market Foundation Garden Grant
description: Funds edible garden programs that connect children to fresh produce and nutrition education. Open to U.S. and Canadian K-12 schools, including nonprofit private schools, that engage at least 10 children regularly in garden programming.
notes: Opens annually in late winter; the 2026 cycle (opened Feb 1, deadline March 15, 2026) is closed. Reopens February 2027.

name: Sprouts Healthy Communities Foundation Grants
description: Supports nutrition education and healthy lifestyle programs for kids, families, and adults, including school garden and cooking programs. Open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits (and schools for the gardens program) in communities where Sprouts Farmers Market operates stores.
notes: 2026 applications are closed; awardees notified August 2026. Next funding cycle opens Spring 2027.

name: RWJF Health Equity Research for Action (HERA)
description: Funds community-centered, action-oriented research conducted in equitable community partnership to advance health equity in the U.S. 501(c)(3) nonprofits are preferred (public entities also eligible), with a required community partnership of at least two years.
notes: This specific call's window has closed (LOI May 14, 2026; full proposal July 27, 2026), but RWJF posts new health-equity calls on a recurring basis. Up to ~20 awards of up to $500,000 each.

name: Tory Burch Foundation Fellows Program
amount: $5,000 business education grant plus a one-year fellowship
description: A year-long fellowship for women entrepreneurs whose for-profit, women-majority-owned U.S. business generates at least $75,000 in annual revenue. Up to 120 fellows receive a grant, coaching, and community access each year.
notes: Recurring annual program. The 2026 cohort deadline was November 11, 2025; applications typically reopen in fall.

name: Cartier Women's Initiative Awards
description: An annual award for women-owned and women-led impact-driven businesses with 1 to 6 years of operation and annual revenue between $50,000 and $5 million. First, second, and third place in each region receive $100,000, $60,000, and $30,000.
notes: Applications for the 2027 edition close June 16, 2026. Recurring annual program.

name: SoGal Black Founder Startup Grant
description: Cash grants for Black women and Black nonbinary entrepreneurs with a legally registered, scalable business who plan to seek investor financing. Awardees also receive fundraising guidance and lifetime access to the SoGal Foundation and SoGal Ventures teams.
notes: Rolling application via email to sogal@iamsogal.com.

name: Galaxy Grant
description: A recurring cash grant from the nonprofit Hidden Star for women and minority business owners at any stage. The application is free and quick, and applicants are automatically entered for future Galaxy Grant cycles.
notes: Current cycle deadline July 31, 2026. Recurring program for women and minority entrepreneurs.

name: Freed Fellowship Grant
amount: $500 monthly grant plus a chance at a $2,500 year-end grant
description: A monthly $500 no-strings-attached grant for micro and small business owners in the U.S., with women, minority, and other underrepresented founders especially encouraged. Monthly fellows are eligible for an additional $2,500 end-of-year grant.
notes: Rolling monthly deadlines (last day of each month). A small application fee applies.

name: Founders First Job Creators Quest Grant
amount: Cash award plus full-tuition accelerator scholarship
description: Grants for diverse-led service businesses or light manufacturers with 2 to 20 employees and $100,000 to $3 million in revenue that can add premium-wage jobs. Diverse founders include Black, Indigenous, people of color, LGBTQIA+, veterans, women, or those in low-to-moderate-income areas.
notes: Recurring regional grant cycles run throughout the year.

name: National Pride Grant
amount: $1,000 grant plus accelerator program access (25 awardees)
description: A grant for LGBTQIA+-led U.S. small businesses with at least one year in business, 2 to 100 employees, and under $5 million in annual revenue. Twenty-five awardees each receive $1,000 plus participation in a business accelerator.
notes: Recurring annual program; applications open around April each year.

name: NGLCC Community Impact Grant Program
funder: National LGBT Chamber of Commerce and Grubhub Community Fund
description: Grants for LGBTQ+-owned or LGBTQ+ ally-owned restaurants, bars, and cafes that serve food, supporting equipment, marketing, staffing, and infrastructure. The program targets 30 percent of funds to businesses owned by people of color and transgender and gender-expansive individuals.
notes: Recurring annual program with Grubhub; reopens for new cycles.

name: StreetShares Foundation Veteran Small Business Award
description: An annual award competition for veteran, reserve, active-duty, or military-spouse entrepreneurs who own at least 50 percent of a business with a social impact on the military veteran community. Winners are selected on business concept, impact, and need.
notes: Recurring annual program; application window typically reopens in October/November.

name: First Peoples Fund Artist in Business Leadership Fellowship
amount: $10,000 fellowship grant plus year-long professional development
description: A year-long fellowship providing independent Native American artists a working-capital grant to build their art-based business, along with professional development and network-building support. Each cohort selects roughly a dozen fellows.
notes: Recurring annual fellowship; 2026 recipients announced in March 2026, applications reopen for the next cycle.

name: AAPISTRONG Small Business Grant
amount: Cash grants commonly $5,000 to $10,000 depending on cycle
description: Cash grants for AAPI-majority-owned (51 percent or more) for-profit U.S. small businesses that have operated for at least three months. Recurring funds, including the Proudly Unstoppable Fund, are offered through National ACE partnerships.
notes: Multiple recurring grant cycles; check current openings on the AAPISTRONG site.

name: Black Ambition Prize
amount: Up to $1,000,000 in prize funding across awardees
description: A national competition funding early-stage businesses with at least one Black, Hispanic/Latino, LGBTQ, first-generation immigrant, or other underrepresented founder, in consumer products, healthcare, media, technology, or AI. Ventures must have raised less than $1 million in dilutive funding.
notes: Recurring annual prize; 2026 applications open. Also incorporates the Dream Makers Founder Grant for underrepresented CPG founders.

name: Coalition to Back Black Businesses Grant
funder: U.S. Chamber of Commerce Foundation and American Express
description: A multi-year initiative awarding grants, mentoring, and coaching to Black-owned small businesses in economically distressed U.S. communities. Selected recipients of the initial grant can later receive a larger enhancement grant.
notes: Recurring program; reopens for new grant rounds.

name: NALAC Fund for the Arts
funder: National Association of Latino Arts and Cultures
amount: Grants of $5,000 for individual artists and ensembles
description: The premier national grant program dedicated to Latinx/e individual artists, cultural workers, ensembles, and arts organizations based in the U.S. and Puerto Rico. Grants support artistic and cultural projects, including those addressing social change.
notes: Recurring annual program; the 2026 individuals and ensembles cycle closed March 23, 2026, and reopens for the next cycle.

name: HerRise MicroGrant
description: Awards a $1,000 microgrant every month to an under-resourced woman-owned small business, including women of color, across a variety of industries. Has run continuously since 2017.
notes: Applications close the last day of each month; a small administrative fee applies. Business must be at least 51% women-owned, U.S.-registered, under $1M revenue.

name: CO—100: America's Top Small Business Awards
amount: $2,000 for 10 category winners; $25,000 for the top business
description: Annual awards program recognizing the top 100 small businesses in America, with cash prizes for category winners and one grand-prize Top Business. Open to U.S. for-profit businesses with under 250 employees and under $20 million in revenue.
notes: Recurring annual program. Deadline July 23, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET.

name: Amex Shop Small Grants Program
funder: American Express and Main Street America
description: Grants of $20,000 to U.S. brick-and-mortar small businesses to fund growth and community-impact projects. Administered by Main Street America in partnership with American Express.
notes: Recurring annual program launched around Small Business Saturday; 2025-2026 cycle closed January 16, 2026. Requires a physical storefront.

name: Intuit Small Business Hero Program
description: Quarterly program spotlighting three U.S. small business owners who demonstrate community impact, awarding each a $20,000 grant plus QuickBooks and Mailchimp resources. Businesses are nominated rather than self-applied.
notes: Recurring quarterly phases. Nomination-based; owners cannot nominate their own business.

name: Bizee Fresh Start Business Grant
description: Grant supporting U.S. entrepreneurs starting or growing a business, providing $2,500 in funding plus a business formation package. Open to U.S. residents aged 21 or older.
notes: Evergreen program for early-stage founders.

name: The Halstead Grant
description: Annual business-development award for emerging U.S. silver jewelry designers who have been in business five years or less. Winner receives start-up capital plus industry recognition; finalists and semi-finalists receive smaller cash prizes.
notes: Recurring annual; call for entries opens each summer. 2026 deadline was May 1, 2026 (passed). U.S. residents/permanent residents only.

name: Etsy Emergency Relief Fund
description: Provides grants to Etsy sellers in the U.S. who have experienced a federally declared disaster within the past year. Administered with the nonprofit CERF+ and awarded on a quarterly, need-based basis.
notes: Rolling/quarterly. Requires being an active seller for at least one year and an account in good standing.

name: DoorDash Local Business Disaster Relief Fund
description: Grants for local restaurants and small businesses that lost revenue due to a recent state of emergency or federally declared natural disaster. Covers expenses such as rent, utilities, supplies, and inventory not covered by insurance.
notes: Recurring with four annual rounds (Summer, Fall, Winter, Spring). Businesses must have 3 or fewer locations, under 50 employees per location, and under $3M revenue per location.

name: American Farmland Trust Brighter Future Fund
description: Grants to farmers and ranchers to improve farm viability, access land, adopt regenerative practices, or build resilience, with priority for beginning, limited-resource, veteran, and historically underserved producers.
notes: Offered through multiple grant tracks during the year; the 2026 National Farm Viability Grant window is June 8-18, 2026.

name: Lenovo Evolve Small AI Grant
amount: $25,000 plus $10,000 in technology and AI coaching
description: Grant championing minority-, disabled-, and woman-owned small businesses, providing cash funding, Lenovo AI PCs and technology, plus a year of business coaching on integrating AI. Open to U.S. and Canada businesses under $7.5M revenue with 75 or fewer employees.
notes: Recurring program; currently between application rounds. Sign up on the site for next-round notification.

name: Eileen Fisher Women-Owned Business Grant
description: Grants to women-owned businesses founded on environmental and social change, supporting positive environmental practices and a sustainable economy, plus a networking event in New York City.
notes: Recurring annual; application window typically runs in April. Requires at least 3 years in business and gross revenue under $1M; must be 100% women-owned.
